description: US President Donald Trump's upcoming China visit, scheduled from March 31 to April 2, will be limited to Beijing due to a packed schedule and security concerns. While Chinese officials initially considered a multi-city itinerary, time constraints and logistical challenges led to the decision to focus solely on the capital.

Actor Ashish Sharma, who previously played Rama, revealed the images were digitally altered from his own show, 'Siya Ke Ram', debunking the 'leaked' photos.

description: Former spinner Harbhajan Singh dismissed notions of the Ahmedabad stadium being unlucky for India. He emphasized that match outcomes hinge solely on player performance, citing Australia's superior play in the 2023 World Cup final.  Harbhajan drew parallels to past perceptions of Eden Gardens, asserting that such beliefs stem from those unfamiliar with cricket's realities.

description: India's T20 World Cup 2026 heroes Ishan Kishan and Abhishek Sharma shared their journeys of overcoming criticism and self-doubt. Kishan highlighted the importance of self-belief and hard work after a two-year absence, while Sharma credited the unwavering support of teammates and management for his crucial final performance. Both emphasized the power of positive company and continuous improvement.

Rs 70k crore tax evasion probe: Madurai to Shimla — raids at over 70 restaurants over ‘vanishing bill’
description: The Income Tax investigation wing has launched a pan-India survey of approximately 70 restaurants suspected of sales suppression totaling Rs 700 crore. This action follows a Rs 70,000 crore probe into billing software manipulation used to evade taxes. Investigations revealed methods like deleting cash transactions and under-declaring revenue.

title: Astros eye $22M Brewers power duo in blockbuster Isaac Paredes trade
description: Houston Astros and Milwaukee Brewers are reportedly discussing a trade that could send infielder Isaac Paredes to Milwaukee for outfielder Garrett Mitchell and pitcher Aaron Ashby. This move addresses Houston's outfield and pitching needs while bolstering Milwaukee's lineup with power. Both teams gain players under team control, making the deal financially viable.

title: Sundar Pichai congratulates Team India on winning the ICC Men's T20 World Cup
description: India achieved a historic T20 World Cup victory, becoming the first nation to defend its title and win on home soil. Their dominant performance against New Zealand, highlighted by exceptional batting and bowling, secured their third T20 World Cup title. Tech leaders Sundar Pichai and Satya Nadella congratulated the team on their remarkable achievement.

title: Salesforce CEO says layoffs at Jack Dorsey's Block do not signal Al-led jobs apocalypse
description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff dismisses Block's massive layoffs as company-specific issues, not an AI-driven job apocalypse. He argues AI enhances productivity without wholesale cuts, citing Salesforce's growth.  Critics suggest Block's cuts are due to pandemic-era bloat, not solely AI, a narrative echoed by former employees and analysts.

title: Dwight Howard’s wife Amy Luciani reveals CPS visits, shocking claims
description: Dwight Howard's wife, Amy Luciani, revealed on Instagram that Child Protective Services visited their home multiple times. This led to their daughter being taken away. She alleges this stems from Dwight's alleged drug addiction and claims he coached their son to lie to police. This follows previous accusations and a brief marriage ending in divorce.

title: Amazon sends complaint letter to FCC against Elon Musk's Starlink
description: Amazon's Leo has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's ambitious one million-satellite proposal, citing a lack of critical orbital and RF data. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, lacks space safety details, and would take centuries to deploy, potentially monopolizing orbital resources. The filing questions the feasibility and intent behind SpaceX's massive constellation.

title: New England Patriots, Colts linked to $50M Bengals DE Trey Hendrickson
description: Sports Illustrated’s Gilberto Manzano projects Cincinnati Bengals defensive end Trey Hendrickson for a three-year, $105M deal with the New England Patriots and Indianapolis Colts as potential landing spots. New England needs pass rush help for their quarterback Drake Maye. And Indianapolis seeks to improve their defense under former Cincinnati defensive coordinator Lou Anarumo.  The Colts may free up cap space by trading or releasing their wide receiver Michael Pittman Jr., who is in the final year of his three-year contract.
